title: Crash on save (Mac, v2.3.1) — likely encoding error from CSV-imported special characters
problem: TaskFlow crashes immediately every time the user hits Save. The app window closes with a briefly-visible error dialog that may reference an encoding issue. The user has ~200 tasks and loses unsaved work on every crash. The problem began after bulk-importing tasks from a CSV file that contained typographic characters (smart/curly quotes, em-dashes, etc.).
root_cause_hypothesis: The save/serialization path does not handle non-ASCII or non-UTF-8 characters (e.g., Windows-1252 smart quotes, em-dashes) that were introduced via CSV import. When the app attempts to serialize the task list to disk, it hits an encoding error on one of these characters and crashes instead of handling the error gracefully.
reproduction_steps: Prepare a CSV file containing tasks with typographic characters: curly/smart quotes (“ ” ‘ ’), em-dashes (—), and other common non-ASCII punctuation
Import the CSV into TaskFlow so the task list is substantial (50+ tasks to approximate the reporter's 200-task scenario)
Edit any task or make any change
Click Save and observe the crash
environment: macOS (version not specified), TaskFlow 2.3.1, desktop app
impact: Complete inability to save any work. The user cannot use the application at all — every save attempt crashes, causing data loss. Any user who has imported CSV data containing non-ASCII characters is likely affected.
recommended_fix: 1. Inspect the save/serialization code path for encoding assumptions (e.g., ASCII-only or latin-1 encoding where UTF-8 is needed). 2. Check the CSV import path — it may be ingesting bytes without normalizing to UTF-8, so the bad encoding is latent until save. 3. Ensure the save path either normalizes all text to UTF-8 or gracefully handles encoding errors rather than crashing. 4. Add a try/catch around the save operation so that even if encoding fails, the user gets a readable error and no data loss. 5. Consider adding a data migration or repair tool that re-encodes existing task data for users already affected.
proposed_test_case: Unit test: create a task with content containing smart quotes (“”), em-dashes (—), and other common Windows-1252 characters. Call the save/serialize function and verify it completes without error and round-trips correctly. Integration test: import a CSV file with mixed encodings, then save and reload the full task list, asserting no data loss or crash.
information_gaps: Exact macOS version not provided (unlikely to affect the fix but worth noting)
No crash log obtained — the exact exception and stack trace are unknown; developers should reproduce to capture this
Unknown whether the crash is tied specifically to CSV-imported tasks or affects all tasks once any problematic data is in the store
Original CSV file encoding (Windows-1252, ISO-8859-1, etc.) not confirmed
